strengths: 15+ years of engineering experience across diverse domains including web, mobile, embedded systems, and infrastructure, satisfying the minimum tenure requirement.
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq signals awareness of the agentic AI space.
Founded and maintains GISCollective as an open-source platform used in production by NGOs, demonstrating some entrepreneurial initiative.
concerns: AI expertise is negligible: the only AI work listed is ~3 months at re:cinq (April 2025–present) building developer tooling; no demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale, no publications, no industry recognition, and no advanced degree in an AI-related field — directly missing every preferred qualification for this dimension.

Cloud experience is superficial: the resume cites AWS CDK at one employer (Optilyz) with no evidence of cloud architecture leadership, multi-cloud strategy, or platform-level thinking — a critical gap for a role centered on cloud and AI platform strategy.

Seniority and scope mismatch is severe: all titles held are Senior or Staff individual contributor; the role is Director-level with responsibilities for advising strategic customers and partners, shaping cloud product futures, and inspiring external audiences — none of which appear anywhere in the candidate's history.
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable hands-on engineer with a long track record in software craftsmanship, but he is a poor fit for this role on its three most critical axes: the Principal Engineer — AI Strategy position demands recognized AI expertise with shipped AI products, and the candidate has roughly three months of AI-adjacent tooling work and no AI credentials; it requires deep cloud platform knowledge, which is absent beyond a single AWS CDK reference; and it is explicitly Director-level, expecting strategic customer advisory and external thought leadership that the candidate has never operated at — his most senior external-facing work is local workshop facilitation. The gap between where the candidate is and what this role demands is too wide to bridge in a screening context.
